content.title: Mixed practice with all measure words
content.body:
Combine all learned measure words in various contexts:

**个 (gè)** — people, general objects  
**本 (běn)** — books  
**杯 (bēi)** — beverages  
**张 (zhāng)** — flat objects

Practice identifying which measure word fits which noun, and use 两 (liǎng) for "two."

content.title: The general measure word 个 (gè)
content.body:
**个 (gè)** is the most common measure word in Chinese. It is used for people and many general objects.

**一个人** (yī gè rén) — one person  
**两个学生** (liǎng gè xuésheng) — two students  
**三个苹果** (sān gè píngguǒ) — three apples

When you're unsure which measure word to use, 个 (gè) is often acceptable

content.title: Building the basic structure
content.body:
The complete structure is: **Number + Measure Word + Noun**

**一个人** (yī gè rén) — 1 + [measure word] + person  
**三本书** (sān běn shū) — 3 + [measure word] + book  
**两杯水** (liǎng bēi shuǐ) — 2 + [measure word] + water

Each part is essential. Removing the measure word makes the phrase grammatically

content.title: Counting multiple items
content.body:
When counting multiple items, the structure remains the same: Number + Measure Word + Noun.

**一个人** (yī gè rén) — one person  
**五个人** (wǔ gè rén) — five people  
**十个人** (shí gè rén) — ten people

**一本书** (yī běn shū) — one book  
**六本书** (liù běn shū) — six books

**一杯水** (yī bēi shuǐ) — one cup

content.title: The number 两 (liǎng) vs 二 (èr) for "two"
content.body:
Chinese has two words for "two": **二 (èr)** and **两 (liǎng)**.

Before measure words, use **两 (liǎng)**:
- **两个人** (liǎng gè rén) — two people
- **两本书** (liǎng běn shū) — two books
- **两杯水** (liǎng bēi shuǐ) — two cups of water

Use 二 (èr) for counting: 一、二、三、四 (yī, èr, sān, sì).

content.title: When to use 个 (gè) as a default
content.body:
While different nouns have specific measure words, **个 (gè)** is the safest choice when you're uncertain.

**Specific measure word:** 一本书 (yī běn shū) — one book  
**Default measure word:** 一个苹果 (yī gè píngguǒ) — one apple

Native speakers will understand you even if you use 个 (gè) instead of a more

content.title: Distinguishing 两 (liǎng) and 二 (èr)
content.body:
**Use 两 (liǎng)** before measure words:
- 两个人 (liǎng gè rén) — two people
- 两本书 (liǎng běn shū) — two books
- 两杯水 (liǎng bēi shuǐ) — two cups of water

**Use 二 (èr)** when counting or in compound numbers like 12, 22, etc.:
- 一、二、三 (yī, èr, sān) — one, two, three
- 十二 (shí èr) — twelve
- 二 is also us

content.title: The measure word 张 (zhāng) for flat objects
content.body:
**张 (zhāng)** is used for flat objects like paper, tables, and beds.

**一张纸** (yī zhāng zhǐ) — one sheet of paper  
**两张桌子** (liǎng zhāng zhuōzi) — two tables  
**三张床** (sān zhāng chuáng) — three beds

Think of 张 as the measure word for things with flat surfaces.

content.title: Translating from English to Chinese
content.body:
Translate complete phrases from English to Chinese, choosing appropriate measure words:

**"four people"** → 四个人 (sì gè rén)  
**"one book"** → 一本书 (yī běn shū)  
**"three cups of tea"** → 三杯茶 (sān bēi chá)  
**"six sheets of paper"** → 六张纸 (liù zhāng zhǐ)

Remember:
1. Choose the correct number wor

content.title: Common mistake: omitting the measure word
content.body:
At HSK 1 level, omitting the measure word is a major grammatical error.

**Incorrect:** ~~三人~~ (sān rén)  
**Correct:** 三个人 (sān gè rén)

**Incorrect:** ~~一书~~ (yī shū)  
**Correct:** 一本书 (yī běn shū)

**At HSK 1 level, incomplete phrases like "四杯" are typically considered errors:** Without the noun

content.title: Creating original sentences
content.body:
Use the number + measure word + noun structure to create your own sentences:

**Pattern:** Subject + 有 (yǒu, "have") + Number + Measure Word + Noun

Examples:
- 我有三本书。(Wǒ yǒu sān běn shū.) — I have three books.
- 他有两个学生。(Tā yǒu liǎng gè xuésheng.) — He has two students.
- 我们有五杯茶。(Wǒmen yǒu wǔ bēi ch

content.title: The measure word 本 (běn) for books
content.body:
**本 (běn)** is the measure word specifically for books, magazines, and similar bound publications.

**一本书** (yī běn shū) — one book  
**两本书** (liǎng běn shū) — two books  
**五本书** (wǔ běn shū) — five books

Note: You cannot say ~~一书~~ or ~~两书~~. The measure word 本 is required.

content.title: Choosing the correct measure word
content.body:
To choose the right measure word, consider the noun's category:

**People and general objects → 个 (gè)**  
一个人 (yī gè rén), 两个学生 (liǎng gè xuésheng), 三个苹果 (sān gè píngguǒ)

**Books and publications → 本 (běn)**  
一本书 (yī běn shū), 两本杂志 (liǎng běn zázhì)

**Beverages in containers → 杯 (bēi)**  
一杯水 (y

content.title: Real-world application scenarios
content.body:
In real situations, you'll need to produce measure word phrases spontaneously:

**At a café:** "I want two cups of coffee."  
我要两杯咖啡。(Wǒ yào liǎng bēi kāfēi.)

**At a bookstore:** "I want to buy three books."  
我要买三本书。(Wǒ yào mǎi sān běn shū.)

**Introducing people:** "I have five students."  
我有五个学

content.title: Numbers 1-10 review
content.body:
Here are the numbers 1-10 in Chinese:

**一 (yī)** — one  
**二 (èr)** — two (counting)  
**三 (sān)** — three  
**四 (sì)** — four  
**五 (wǔ)** — five  
**六 (liù)** — six  
**七 (qī)** — seven  
**八 (bā)** — eight  
**九 (jiǔ)** — nine  
**十 (shí)** — ten

Remember: use 两 (liǎng) instead of 二 (èr) before

content.title: What is a measure word?
content.body:
In Chinese, you cannot directly put a number before a noun. You need a **measure word** (量词 liàngcí) between them.

**English:** three books  
**Chinese:** 三**本**书 (sān **běn** shū)

The measure word (本 běn in this example) comes between the number and the noun. Different nouns use different measure

content.title: Error correction practice
content.body:
Identify and correct common errors in measure word usage:

**Incorrect:** ~~五书~~ → **Correct:** 五本书 (wǔ běn shū)  
(Missing measure word)

**Incorrect:** ~~二个人~~ → **Correct:** 两个人 (liǎng gè rén)  
(Wrong form of "two")

**Incorrect:** ~~一个书~~ → **Better:** 一本书 (yī běn shū)  
(Wrong measure word, th

content.title: Comprehensive error analysis
content.body:
Analyze sentences containing multiple types of errors:

**Sentence:** ~~我有二个书。~~

**Errors:**
1. 二 should be 两 before measure words
2. 个 is not the specific measure word for books (should be 本)

**Correct:** 我有两本书。(Wǒ yǒu liǎng běn shū.)

Understanding why errors occur helps you avoid them in your o

content.title: Multiple objects with different measure words
content.body:
When listing multiple types of objects, each needs its own appropriate measure word:

**三个人和两本书** (sān gè rén hé liǎng běn shū)  
three people and two books

**一杯茶和一杯咖啡** (yī bēi chá hé yī bēi kāfēi)  
one cup of tea and one cup of coffee

**两张纸和五个苹果** (liǎng zhāng zhǐ hé wǔ gè píngguǒ)  
two sheets

content.title: The measure word 杯 (bēi) for cups/glasses
content.body:
**杯 (bēi)** is the measure word for beverages in cups or glasses.

**一杯水** (yī bēi shuǐ) — one cup/glass of water  
**两杯茶** (liǎng bēi chá) — two cups of tea  
**三杯咖啡** (sān bēi kāfēi) — three cups of coffee

The word 杯 itself means "cup" or "glass," and it functions as a measure word to quantify th
